title: Indus Valley Civilization
content: Also called Harappan Civilization (3300-1300 BCE). Located along Indus River (present Pakistan and northwest India). Major cities: Mohenjo-daro, Harappa, Lothal, Dholavira, Kalibangan. Features: grid-pattern streets, drainage systems, Great Bath, granaries. People knew agriculture, traded with Mesopotamia. Used standardized weights and measures. Script still undeciphered. Declined possibly due to floods, climate change, or invasions.

title: Vedic Period and Maurya Empire
content: Vedic Period (1500-500 BCE): Aryans composed Rigveda, Yajurveda, Samaveda, Atharvaveda. Upanishads: philosophical texts. Caste system (varna) began. Maurya Empire (322-185 BCE): Founded by Chandragupta Maurya with help of Chanakya (Kautilya). Ashoka the Great: grandson of Chandragupta. After Kalinga War, embraced Buddhism. Built pillars with edicts, Lion Capital at Sarnath (Indian emblem). Spread Buddhism to Sri Lanka, Central Asia.

title: Gupta Empire - Golden Age
content: Gupta Empire (320-550 CE): Golden Age of India. Founded by Sri Gupta, expanded by Chandragupta I. Samudragupta: greatest warrior, 'Napoleon of India'. Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya): peak of empire. Achievements: Aryabhata discovered zero, calculated Earth's rotation, pi. Kalidasa wrote Shakuntala, Meghaduta. Nalanda University: world's first residential university. Sushruta: father of surgery. Iron Pillar of Delhi (rust-resistant). Ajanta and Ellora caves art flourished.

title: Mughal Empire
content: Mughal Empire (1526-1857). Babur: first Mughal emperor, won Battle of Panipat (1526). Akbar (1556-1605): greatest Mughal, policy of 'Sulh-i-Kul' (universal peace), Din-i-Ilahi, abolished Jizya tax, land revenue system (Todar Mal). Jahangir: patron of arts. Shah Jahan: built Taj Mahal (1632), Red Fort, Jama Masjid. Aurangzeb: expanded empire to largest extent, but strict policies led to decline. Last Mughal: Bahadur Shah Zafar (exiled after 1857 revolt).

title: Indian Independence Movement
content: British East India Company: trade → rule (1757 Battle of Plassey). 1857 Revolt: First War of Independence (Mangal Pandey, Rani Lakshmibai, Tantia Tope). Indian National Congress: 1885 (A.O. Hume). Muslim League: 1906. Mahatma Gandhi: Non-cooperation (1920), Salt March/Dandi March (1930), Quit India (1942). Subhas Chandra Bose: INA, 'Give me blood, I'll give you freedom'. Bhagat Singh: revolutionary. Partition: August 14/15, 1947. Constitution: January 26, 1950 (Republic Day).

title: World War I and II
content: World War I (1914-1918): Allied Powers (UK, France, Russia, USA) vs Central Powers (Germany, Austria-Hungary, Ottoman Empire). Causes: assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand, imperialism, nationalism. Treaty of Versailles ended WWI. World War II (1939-1945): Allies (UK, USA, USSR, France) vs Axis (Germany, Italy, Japan). Hitler's Nazi Germany invaded Poland. Holocaust: 6 million Jews killed. Hiroshima and Nagasaki atomic bombs (1945). UN founded (1945). Cold War followed between USA and USSR.

title: India - Geography and States
content: India: 7th largest country, 2nd most populated (140+ crore). Area: 3.28 million km². 28 states, 8 union territories. Capital: New Delhi. Borders: Pakistan (west), China/Nepal/Bhutan (north), Bangladesh/Myanmar (east), Sri Lanka (south). Himalayas in north, Thar Desert in west, Deccan Plateau in south. Major rivers: Ganga (longest in India), Yamuna, Brahmaputra, Godavari, Krishna, Narmada. Seasons: Summer (March-June), Monsoon (July-September), Winter (October-February).

title: Indian Constitution and Government
content: Indian Constitution: longest written constitution, adopted Jan 26, 1950. Dr. B.R. Ambedkar: chief architect. Preamble: sovereign, socialist, secular, democratic, republic. Fundamental Rights: equality, freedom, religion, education, constitutional remedies. DPSP: guidelines for governance. Legislature: Parliament (Lok Sabha + Rajya Sabha). Executive: President, PM, Council of Ministers. Judiciary: Supreme Court, High Courts, District Courts. Federal structure with 3 lists: Union, State, Concurrent. 3-tier Panchayati Raj: Gram Panchayat, Block, District.

title: Earth, Continents and Oceans
content: Earth: 3rd planet from Sun. Age: ~4.5 billion years. 71% water, 29% land. Rotation: 24 hours (day/night). Revolution: 365.25 days (seasons). Tilt: 23.5°. 7 continents: Asia (largest), Africa, North America, South America, Antarctica, Europe, Australia (smallest). 5 oceans: Pacific (largest), Atlantic, Indian, Southern, Arctic. Tectonic plates cause earthquakes and volcanoes. Layers: crust, mantle, outer core, inner core. Atmosphere: nitrogen (78%), oxygen (21%).

title: Climate, Weather and Natural Disasters
content: Weather: short-term atmospheric conditions. Climate: long-term weather patterns. Climate zones: tropical, temperate, polar, arid, Mediterranean. Monsoon: seasonal wind bringing rain to South Asia. Global warming: increase in Earth's average temperature due to greenhouse gases (CO₂, methane). Greenhouse effect: gases trap heat like a greenhouse. Natural disasters: earthquakes (Richter scale), tsunamis, cyclones/hurricanes, floods, droughts, volcanic eruptions. Disaster management: early warning systems, evacuation plans, relief operations.
